headline: Exclusive-Rivian mulls making its own lidar sensors, possibly in partnership with Chinese firms
summary: Rivian Automotive is considering making its own lidar sensors and could do so in partnership with a Chinese firm, CEO ‌RJ Scaringe said in an interview with Reuters on Tuesday.  Rivian said that a version of its R2 vehicles coming later this year would include lidar sensors, which help self-driving vehicles gain a three-dimensional view of the road.  Rivian did not disclose who would supply it with lidar sensors, which on demonstration vehicles were much smaller than the large, spinning units found on the streets of San Francisco and other cities in ‌robotaxis designed by Alphabet's Waymo.

content.title: Exclusive-Rivian mulls making its own lidar sensors, possibly in partnership with Chinese firms

headline: NextLadder Ventures Announces Co-Founder Leadership Team, Investment Focus Areas For Over $1 Billion Initiative Empowering Americans with Personalized, Tech-Enabled Support Tools
summary: NextLadder Ventures, a new fund backed by more than $1 billion in capital, today announced its priority investment areas for building a new market for "Navigation Technology" (NavTech) — tools that provide Americans with personalized solutions to navigate life's challenges and achieve greater economic mobility — and announced its co-founding team, including two new senior hires.

headline: Medable CEO Michelle Longmire Featured as Guest Speaker with Leaders from Google and Mayo Clinic at Japan’s Largest Pharmaceutical Summit
summary: PALO ALTO, Calif., May 06, 2026--Medable Inc., the leading technology platform for agentic AI clinical development, today announced that its CEO and co-founder, Dr. Michelle Longmire, will be one of three international guest speakers at Japan’s largest pharmaceutical conference, Ubie Pharma Summit (May 13-14, 2026). Longmire will be speaking at the fifth annual event along with Justin Chen, MD, Clinical Specialist at Google for Health, and John Halamka, MD, President of Mayo Clinic Platform.

content.title: Medable CEO Michelle Longmire Featured as Guest Speaker with Leaders from Google and Mayo Clinic at Japan’s Largest Pharmaceutical Summit
content.summary: PALO ALTO, Calif., May 06, 2026--Medable Inc., the leading technology platform for agentic AI clinical development, today announced that its CEO and co-founder, Dr. Michelle Longmire, will be one of three international guest speakers at Japan’s largest pharmaceutical conference, Ubie Pharma Summit (May 13-14, 2026). Longmire will be speaking at the fifth annual event along with Justin Chen, MD, Clinical Specialist at Google for Health, and John Halamka, MD, President of Mayo Clinic Platform.

headline: NiSource Announces First Quarter Results
summary: MERRILLVILLE, Ind., May 06, 2026--NiSource Inc. (NYSE: NI) today announced, on a GAAP basis, net income available to common shareholders for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 of $510.7 million, or $1.06 of earnings per diluted share, compared to net income available to common shareholders of $474.8 million, or $1.00 of earnings per diluted share, for the same period of 2025.

content.title: NiSource Announces First Quarter Results
content.summary: MERRILLVILLE, Ind., May 06, 2026--NiSource Inc. (NYSE: NI) today announced, on a GAAP basis, net income available to common shareholders for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 of $510.7 million, or $1.06 of earnings per diluted share, compared to net income available to common shareholders of $474.8 million, or $1.00 of earnings per diluted share, for the same period of 2025.

headline: Schneider Electric to launch Southeast Asia training hub in Malaysia
summary: French energy technology firm Schneider Electric plans to open a Southeast Asian training centre in ‌Malaysia this year, a senior executive said on Wednesday, as ‌a boom in artificial intelligence infrastructure drives up power demand in the region.  Southeast Asia's ​data centre capacity is expected to grow threefold by 2030, according to analysts, with Malaysia emerging as a major hotspot, attracting investments from tech giants such as Microsoft, Amazon and Alphabet's Google in recent years.

content.title: Schneider Electric to launch Southeast Asia training hub in Malaysia
